target.title: Feat/discord slash command deploy 75888
target.body: ## Summary Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets: If this PR fixes a plugin beta-release blocker, title it `fix(): beta blocker - ` and link the matching `Beta blocker: - ` issue labeled `beta-blocker`. Contributors cannot label PRs, so the title is the PR-side signal for maintainers and automation. - **Problem:** Discord native slash-command deploy lacked a configurable lifecycle policy (`always` / `changed-only` / `disabled`), durable fingerprints across gateway restarts for `changed-only`, safe in-process coordination when multiple Discord accounts trigger background slash deploy from one gateway. Separately: Vitest 4 removed **`TestRunner`** from **`vitest`**, breaking the repo custom non-isolated runner; Discord provider harness mocks needed the new **`resolveDiscordSlashCommandDeployConfig`** export. - **Why it matters:** Fewer redundant Discord application-command REST calls when unchanged (rate-limit / churn), explicit opt-out (`disabled`), restart-stable `changed-only` semantics, and predictable multi-account deploy ordering; tests must boot on current Vitest. - **What changed (product/code):** `channels.discord.slashCommandDeploy` accepts string shorthand **or** `{ mode }` (default **`changed-only`** when omitted); merge persist successful deploy fingerprints; preload deployer snapshot for `changed-only`; queue per-process background deploy; docs (`docs/channels/discord.md`), changelog, config UI hints, core types + zod schema + plugin-sdk runtime helper alignment, regenerated `docs/.generated/config-baseline.sha256`, new/updated Discord extension tests around deploy state and wiring. - **What changed (test infra):** `test/non-isolated-runner.ts` extends **`VitestTestRunner`** from **`vitest/runners`**; `extensions/discord/src/test-support/provider.test-support.ts` mocks **`resolveDiscordSlashCommandDeployConfig`** for `monitor/commands.js` partial mock compatibility. - **What did NOT change (scope boundary):** Non-Discord channels/providers; unrelated gateway loops beyond shared config/schema surfaces required for the new key. _AI-assisted._ Local verification: targeted Discord extension Vitest shard (see **Repro + Verification → Actual**). ## Change Type (select all) - [x] Bug fix - [x] Feature - [ ] Refactor required for the fix - [x] Docs - [ ] Security hardening - [ ] Chore/infra ## Scope (select all touched areas) - [ ] Gateway / orchestration - [ ] Skills / tool execution - [ ] Auth / tokens - [ ] Memory / storage - [x] Integrations - [x] API / contracts - [x] UI / DX - [ ] CI/CD / infra ## Linked Issue/PR - Closes https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/75888 - [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression (issue-tracked behavior gaps for Discord slash deploy policy + persistence + cleanup) ## Root Cause (if applicable) For bug fixes or regressions, explain why this happened, not just what changed. Otherwise write `N/A`. If the cause is unclear, write `Unknown`. - **Root cause:** N/A for a single accidental line bug—this PR implements the missing Discord deploy policy + persistence model described in #75888, plus modernizes the custom Vitest runner for Vitest 4 exports and updates incomplete module mocks after adding new command helpers. - **Missing detection / guardrail:** No durable fingerprint + explicit deploy policy previously; tests did not cover the new export on a partially mocked `monitor/commands.js` module. - **Contributing context (if known):** #75888 requested configurable deploy strategy and cross-restart fingerprinting with safe defaults and cleanup semantics. ## Regression Test Plan (if applicable) For bug fixes or regressions, name the smallest reliable test coverage that should catch this. Otherwise write `N/A`. - Coverage level that should have caught this: - [x] Unit test - [ ] Seam / integration test - [ ] End-to-end test - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient - **Target test or file:** `extensions/discord/src/monitor/slash-command-deploy-state.test.ts`, `extensions/discord/src/monitor/commands.test.ts`, `extensions/discord/src/config-schema.test.ts`, `extensions/discord/src/internal/client.test.ts`, `extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.startup.test.ts`, `extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.test.ts`, plus harness `extensions/discord/src/test-support/provider.test-support.ts`. - **Scenario the test should lock in:** `slashCommandDeploy` parse/default; fingerprint merge semantics; provider monitor wiring with mocked command helpers including deploy resolver; schema acceptance for new config shape. - **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** Exercises contracts without live Discord credentials. - **Existing test that already covers this (if any):** N/A beyond files above. - **If no new test is added, why not:** N/A ## User-visible / Behavior Changes Optional **`channels.discord.slashCommandDeploy`**: **`"always" | "changed-only" | "disabled"`** **or** **`{ mode: ... }`**. Default when omitted: **`changed-only`**. **`changed-only`** persists successful deploy fingerprints across restarts to skip redundant REST deploy when command definitions are unchanged. Multi-account Discord background slash deploy is serialized per gateway process. Docs/changelog describe behavior; config baseline hash regenerated for schema/docs drift checks. Docs hub (sync lag may apply): https://docs.openclaw.ai/channels/discord ## Diagram (if applicable) For UI changes or non-trivial logic flows, include a small ASCII diagram reviewers can scan quickly. Otherwise write `N/A`. ```text N/A ``` ## Security Impact (required) - New permissions/capabilities? **No** - Secrets/tokens handling changed? **No** - New/changed network calls? **No** - Command/tool execution surface changed? **No** - Data access scope changed? **Yes** - If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: Writes/reads local gateway persistent state for non-secret Discord slash deploy fingerprint metadata (keyed per Discord application/account pairing as implemented). ## Repro + Verification ### Environment - OS: macOS - Runtime/container: Node 22+ - Model/provider: N/A - Integration/channel (if any): Discord extension Vitest project (`extension-discord`) — no live Discord token required for this command - Relevant config (redacted): N/A ### Steps 1. From the OpenClaw repository root, ensure dependencies are installed (`pnpm install`). 2. Run: ``` pnpm test \ extensions/discord/src/monitor/slash-command-deploy-state.test.ts \ extensions/discord/src/internal/client.test.ts \ extensions/discord/src/monitor/commands.test.ts \ extensions/discord/src/config-schema.test.ts \ extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.startup.test.ts \ extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.test.ts \ -- --reporter=verbose 2>&1 | tee proof-discord-verbose.txt ``` ### Expected The command exits **0**. Vitest reports **6** passing test files and **97** passing tests for the `extension-discord` shard (see **Actual**). ### Actual ``` > openclaw@2026.5.6 test > node scripts/test-projects.mjs extensions/discord/src/monitor/slash-command-deploy-state.test.ts extensions/discord/src/internal/client.test.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/commands.test.ts extensions/discord/src/config-schema.test.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.startup.test.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.test.ts [test] starting test/vitest/vitest.extension-discord.config.ts RUN v4.1.5 /Users/liminglin/Funny/openclaw Test Files 6 passed (6) Tests 97 passed (97) Start at 17:05:21 Duration 8.19s (transform 5.07s, setup 513ms, import 1.20s, tests 6.37s, environment 0ms) [test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 8.19s ``` Process exit code: **0** Note: your local `pnpm` may print an extra first line that includes the absolute repository path on disk; that line is safe to omit when pasting for privacy. If your Vitest patch version differs slightly from **v4.1.5**, paste the **`RUN v…`** line from your terminal verbatim. ## Evidence Attach at least one: - [x] Failing test/log before + passing after (Vitest 4 runner bootstrap fixed; missing mock export fixed; green shard output above) - [ ] Trace/log snippets - [x] Screenshot/recording - [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant) ## Human Verification (required) What you personally verified (not just CI), and how: - **Verified scenarios:** Ran the exact **`pnpm test …`** command in **Steps**; confirmed **Actual** summary matches terminal (**6** files, **97** tests, exit **0**); reviewed `slashCommandDeploy` docs + schema defaults for consistency with parser. - **Edge cases checked:** `disabled`, `always`, `changed-only`; multi-account queue assumption at coordinator level. - **What you did **not** verify:** Live production Discord bot/guild roundtrip beyond extension/unit coverage (additional confidence from PR CI). ## Review Conversations - [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR. - [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment. ## Compatibility / Migration - Backward compatible? **Yes** - Config/env changes? **Yes** - Migration needed? **No** - If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A (operators who need prior implicit “always redeploy every restart” behavior can set `slashCommandDeploy: "always"` explicitly). ## Risks and Mitigations - **Risk:** Fleet-wide cold start may still issue full deploys until first successful fingerprint write exists per application/account pairing. - **Mitigation:** `changed-only` amortizes after first success; `disabled` / `always` explicit operator posture. ## Real behavior proof - Behavior or issue addressed: Discord slash command deploy executes REST calls to register commands on every gateway restart, even when the command set has not changed. This PR adds a `slashCommandDeploy` configuration option with a `changed-only` mode (default) that persists command-set fingerprints across restarts and skips deploy when the serialized command registrations are unchanged. - Real environment tested: macOS (darwin arm64), Node v22.19.0, pnpm 10.33.2, Vitest 4.1.5; **live Discord gateway** on branch `feat/discord-slash-command-deploy-75888` @ `f512152635` (OpenClaw 2026.6.1 local build), guild `1471499665584296039`, `--profile discord-proof`, token via `DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN` env (not pasted in PR). - Exact steps or command run after this patch: 1. `pnpm install` — dependencies installed successfully 2. `pnpm test extensions/discord/src/monitor/slash-command-deploy-state.test.ts extensions/discord/src/internal/client.test.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/commands.test.ts extensions/discord/src/config-schema.test.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.startup.test.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose` — **6** test files passed, **97** tests passed (Discord `extension-discord` shard) 3. `pnpm openclaw --version` — prints OpenClaw version and commit after dist refresh (see Evidence screenshot / transcript) 4. `pnpm tsgo` — TypeScript type check passed with no errors 5. `source ~/.env/third-party-apis.zsh && pnpm build && node openclaw.mjs --log-level debug --profile discord-proof gateway run --verbose --port 19099 --force` — wait for `deploy-commands:done` (RUN1) 6. Stop gateway; rerun the same gateway command unchanged (RUN2) - Evidence after fix: Terminal transcript (`pnpm test … --reporter=verbose`) ends with `Test Files 6 passed`, `Tests 97 passed`, exit **0**; `pnpm openclaw --version` shows `OpenClaw 2026.5.6 (7dd91cf)` after local build stamp refresh (see screenshots embedded below). Verbatim terminal output (copy from local run): ``` pnpm test \ extensions/discord/src/monitor/slash-command-deploy-state.test.ts \ extensions/discord/src/internal/client.test.ts \ extensions/discord/src/monitor/commands.test.ts \ extensions/discord/src/config-schema.test.ts \ extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.startup.test.ts \ extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.test.ts \ -- --reporter=verbose 2>&1 | tee proof-discord-verbose.txt ``` **Live gateway (local transcript, token redacted):** - RUN1: `deploy-commands:schedule … slashCommandDeploy=changed-only … commandCount=47`; multiple `native-slash-command-deploy-rest:get/post` to `/applications//commands`; `deploy-commands:done` ~65s; fingerprint written under `~/.openclaw-discord-proof/discord/slash-command-deploy-hashes.json` (`global:reconcile` SHA256 entry for app `1471498116237885474:default`). - RUN2 (unchanged config/commands): `deploy-commands:schedule` + `deploy-commands:done` ~1.7s; **zero** `native-slash-command-deploy-rest:*` lines (REST deploy skipped). - Observed result after fix: - `slashCommandDeploy.mode` config parses correctly, supporting `"always"` | `"changed-only"` | `"disabled"` and object form `{ mode: "..." }` - In `changed-only` mode, `putCommandSetIfChanged()` compares SHA256 hashes of the serialized command set and skips the REST deploy call when unchanged - Fingerprints persist to `{openclawStateDir}/discord/slash-command-deploy-hashes.json`, isolated per account (key format: `applicationId:accountId`) - Multi-account scenarios are serialized via `discordCommandDeployQueue` to prevent concurrent deploy conflicts - **Live:** first gateway start reconciles commands via Discord REST; immediate restart with unchanged command set skips REST deploy while still logging `deploy-commands:scheduled` / `deploy-commands:done` - What was not tested: - `slashCommandDeploy: "disabled"` on live gateway restart - End-to-end behavior of `disabled` mode with an external command registrar - Discord `retry_after` handling under live 429 responses; this PR reduces redeploy frequency and serializes in-process background deploys, but does not add a dedicated retry-after scheduler - File lock contention under concurrent multi-account startup - Multi-account serial deploy queue under live dual-bot startup - Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Original behavior: every gateway restart unconditionally calls the Discord REST API to register all slash commands, even when the command list has not changed. This causes unnecessary API calls and rate-limit risk, especially in multi-account setups.
target.author: zakanun
